checks.a_analysis: The output text accurately reflects the content of the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or section headings were found. The title and image alt text are appropriate and allowed per the 'Acceptable Changes' criteria.
The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output correctly reflects this and does not introduce any fabricated contact information.

All sections from both page 1 and page 2 of the source document are present in the output. This includes 'Staffing & Operations Management', 'Documentation & Record Keeping', 'Technical Support & Infrastructure', and 'Emergency Operations Coordination'.
The source document does not contain any tables; it uses a checklist format which has been correctly converted into lists. Therefore, this check is not applicable and is marked as PASS.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output contains all the instructional text and checklist items found in the source. While the footer text 'Last Update: 7/2025' and 'Page | X' is not explicitly present in the body text elements, the page count is reflected in the metadata, and footer/metadata handling is an acceptable change. No significant body text is omitted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the source document with high fidelity. Even a spelling error in the source ('milage') was correctly preserved in the output. There are no changes in wording that alter the meaning of the document.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON correctly represents the document content without duplicating the repeated headers (logos and titles) found on the second page of the source PDF. Each checklist item and section heading appears exactly once in the structured output.

The JSON structure uses a nested section/subsection model that maintains a logical hierarchy. The main committee name is the top-level section title, the checklist title is an H2 within that section, and the thematic groups (e.g., 'Staffing & Operations Management') are organized as subsections, which would logically correspond to H3 elements. There are no skipped levels.
There are no tables in the source document. The checklist items are correctly identified as lists, and the header fields (Year, Organization, Contact) are treated as a paragraph block.

The JSON follows the logical flow of the document, processing the content of Page 1 from top to bottom before appending the content of Page 2 (the 'Emergency Operations Coordination' section). This matches the intended reading order of the checklist.
The checkboxes in the source PDF are correctly mapped to unordered list items in the JSON. The number of items in each list (3, 3, 2, and 4) matches the source document perfectly.
There are no explicit hyperlinks in the source document. The QR code is represented as an image element, and no fake links have been injected into the text.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the overall visual organization of the source document. The document title and section headings follow the same logical order. Although the multi-page layout of the PDF is converted to a single scrolling HTML page, and checklist checkboxes are converted to bullet points, these are expected differences. The form labels ('Year:', 'Organization:', 'Form Contact:') have lost their vertical spacing and line breaks, appearing on a single line, but this does not fundamentally confuse the meaning of the document sections.
There are no tables in the source PDF document to evaluate.

The source PDF contains meaningful logos in the header of both pages, including the 'Hurri Up' logo (which features a graphic of North Carolina with a hurricane symbol and the text 'Hurri Up') and the 'LGC' logo. While the QR code image from the bottom of the PDF pages is correctly rendered at the top of the HTML, the branding logos from the header are missing entirely from the HTML output. These are meaningful images that establish the identity and context of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Logos for 'Hurri Up' and 'LGC' appearing in the header area on pages 1 and 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows no images corresponding to the 'Hurri Up' or 'LGC' logos at the top of the document; only the QR code is present.
checks.b_evidence_location: Document header, corresponding to PDF pages 1 and 2.

checks.a_analysis: There is no visual duplication of body content such as paragraphs, checklist items, or sections. While the document title and certain header phrases appear twice at the beginning of the HTML, this is consistent with the provided instructions regarding document titles appearing in both navigation/header elements and content bodies, and is not a defect.
